steps.title: Identify what researchers do with fossil evidence
steps.content: The passage states that PSP structures don't fossilize directly, but researchers believe they were present and look for observable indicators like foramina. The blank needs a word for how they move from these indirect clues to determining whether air sacs existed.

steps.title: Apply the meaning to the choices
steps.content: Infer means to reach a logical conclusion from available evidence—exactly what researchers do when they spot holes in fossils and conclude that air sacs were present. The other three describe different processes that don't fit this evidence-to-conclusion dynamic. That matches choice A — A is correct.
